name: Alexander, Andrew Nurse
motto: "Cursed be he that moves my bones."
description: Zeke knows how to move those bones on the football field, and it is current opinion that if he had wanted it very badly he might have been wearing his "M" at present. Like the immortal "Buddy," Zeke is musical, but his muse seeks expression in tenor wailings instead of piano performances. In the case of a fellow whom everybody likes as well as they do Zeke, there is really not much need for the historic inscription, set on her own photograph by a fair hand—"Lest You Forget."
text:
"Cursed be he that moves my bones."
Zeke knows how to move those bones on the football field, and it is current opinion that if he had wanted it very badly he might have been wearing his "M" at present. Like the immortal "Buddy," Zeke is musical, but his muse seeks expression in tenor wailings instead of piano performances. In the case of a fellow whom everybody likes as well as they do Zeke, there is really not much need for the historic inscription, set on her own photograph by a fair hand—"Lest You Forget."

name: Allen, Junius Wayne
motto: "A politician—one that would circumvent God."
clubs: Member of the Winning Team in Mississippi-Arkansas Pentagonal Debate, 1912
Member of Winning Team in Inter-Society Debate, 1912
Board of Control of the Mississippian, 1912-1913
Winner Grand Gold Medal in State W. C. T. U. Contest
description: Very singular that this section of Prentiss transplanted in Covington should choose to be a politician, for everything he has got in the University has been come by honestly. From which it may be deduced that he loves politics for its excitement and cultivates it as a hobby, while he prefers to go after the things he has to have in a straightforward way.
text:
"A politician—one that would circumvent God."
Very singular that this section of Prentiss transplanted in Covington should choose to be a politician, for everything he has got in the University has been come by honestly. From which it may be deduced that he loves politics for its excitement and cultivates it as a hobby, while he prefers to go after the things he has to have in a straightforward way.

name: Boyett, Reuben Watson
motto: "Still treads upon the heels of pleasure."
description: A chemical wonder who can cause explosions, and compound unspeakable smells with the very best. We firmly believe that we shall see some day Rube's picture in the scientific papers for having discovered a smell more horrible than all those which went before.
text:
"Still treads upon the heels of pleasure."
A chemical wonder who can cause explosions, and compound unspeakable smells with the very best. We firmly believe that we shall see some day Rube's picture in the scientific papers for having discovered a smell more horrible than all those which went before.

name: Bransford, Bettie Lou
motto: "The mildest manners and the gentlest heart."
description: One who can still have this said about her, after four years of acquaintance, must surely have these good qualities for keeps. And her industry is equal to her geniality.
text:
"The mildest manners and the gentlest heart."
One who can still have this said about her, after four years of acquaintance, must surely have these good qualities for keeps. And her industry is equal to her geniality.

name: Broome, W. L.
motto: "A man he was to all the country dear."
description: His sphere of usefulness extends all over this and neighboring counties, and he has as much genuine, useful college spirit as any man in school.
text:
"A man he was to all the country dear."
His sphere of usefulness extends all over this and neighboring counties, and he has as much genuine, useful college spirit as any man in school.

name: Buchanan, James Richardson
motto: "If there be, or ever were one such, its past the size of dreaming."
description: "Little Buck" will stay on after us and try his strength with "Uncle Tommie." Judging by what he has done in lit., we think that he will pass unscratched through the fiery ordeal, and survive to frighten Brandon with his legal thunders.
text:
"If there be, or ever were one such, its past the size of dreaming."
"Little Buck" will stay on after us and try his strength with "Uncle Tommie." Judging by what he has done in lit., we think that he will pass unscratched through the fiery ordeal, and survive to frighten Brandon with his legal thunders.

name: Carpenter, James Madison
description: One who uses his time well, for he studies all week and on Sunday goes out in the surrounding country to preach and do good. If he does as well at his preaching as at his studying, we are sure that the whole country will call him blessed, and that the stars in his crown will be far brighter and more numerous than in most peoples.
text: One who uses his time well, for he studies all week and on Sunday goes out in the surrounding country to preach and do good. If he does as well at his preaching as at his studying, we are sure that the whole country will call him blessed, and that the stars in his crown will be far brighter and more numerous than in most peoples.

name: Benjamin, H. Deane
motto: "Here is one Frenchman more."
description: This inscription ought by rights to be in French, for Mr. Deister will agree that Dean has us all beat in the "parlez-vous" line. He is likewise one of the tribe of musicians who have come to the front this year, and who help fill the aching void that Buddy left.
text:
"Here is one Frenchman more."
This inscription ought by rights to be in French, for Mr. Deister will agree that Dean has us all beat in the "parlez-vous" line. He is likewise one of the tribe of musicians who have come to the front this year, and who help fill the aching void that Buddy left.

name: Doxey, Wall
motto: "In alle the ordres foure is none that can So muchel of daliaunce and fair language."
description: It took blithesome Chaucer to do it. The same sort of fellow that the old poet had in mind when he began, "A frere there was, a wantowne and a merye, A lymtour, a full solempe man, et supra,"—one of those fellows who wants a lot of things and who knows the value of a good tongue in getting them. Ask him—if you have nothing to advertise.
text:
"In alle the ordres foure is none that can So muchel of daliaunce and fair language."
It took blithesome Chaucer to do it. The same sort of fellow that the old poet had in mind when he began, "A frere there was, a wantowne and a merye, A lymtour, a full solempe man, et supra,"—one of those fellows who wants a lot of things and who knows the value of a good tongue in getting them. Ask him—if you have nothing to advertise.

name: Farley, Grace
motto: "With shining morning face Creeping along unwillingly to school."
description: One of the few girls who have taken Taylor medals at the University, Miss Farley yet does not let her learning sit heavily enough on her soul to lessen her graciousness or dim her smile.
text:
"With shining morning face Creeping along unwillingly to school."
One of the few girls who have taken Taylor medals at the University, Miss Farley yet does not let her learning sit heavily enough on her soul to lessen her graciousness or dim her smile.

name: Franklin, Cornell Sidney
motto: "The glass of fashion and the mold of form."
description: An all-round man whose record is clean and bright alike in the class-room, on the athletic field, as post-master, as social "light," and in the closer associations of friendship. He will make a splendid lawyer, and besides, expects to be the first governor of the future state of Texalette.
text:
"The glass of fashion and the mold of form."
An all-round man whose record is clean and bright alike in the class-room, on the athletic field, as post-master, as social "light," and in the closer associations of friendship. He will make a splendid lawyer, and besides, expects to be the first governor of the future state of Texalette.

name: Gibson, Joseph Edward
motto: "Were I a word-mosaic artificer ... ."
description: A man who has endeavored, with more or less success and satisfaction, to make his vocabulary comprehend the whole English language. Often his speech has been heard to groan in the expression of an idea as does an eight-wheel log wagon under the terrific weight of a pine burr. Possessor of a good thinking organ, energy to keep it at work, and self-confidence to direct it, his chances will be excellent for success in any of the professions for which these characteristics fit him.
text:
"Were I a word-mosaic artificer ... ."
A man who has endeavored, with more or less success and satisfaction, to make his vocabulary comprehend the whole English language. Often his speech has been heard to groan in the expression of an idea as does an eight-wheel log wagon under the terrific weight of a pine burr. Possessor of a good thinking organ, energy to keep it at work, and self-confidence to direct it, his chances will be excellent for success in any of the professions for which these characteristics fit him.

name: Gillespie, G. Y.
motto: "None but himself can be his parallel."
description: The last but two from Duck Hill will never break a speed limit, but he'll get there. He has proven the saying that one will be repaid in terms of the effort put forth, and when he has had his other two years he will indeed be a doctor.
text:
"None but himself can be his parallel."
The last but two from Duck Hill will never break a speed limit, but he'll get there. He has proven the saying that one will be repaid in terms of the effort put forth, and when he has had his other two years he will indeed be a doctor.

name: Graves, Robert Preston
motto: "Was man an der Natur Geheimnisvolles pries, Das wagen wir verständig zu probiren, Und was sie sonst organisiren liess, Das lassen wir Krystallisiren."
description: In the scale of his affections stand in order, Chemistry, biology, and the ladies. Although he would use the first person plural "wir" out of deference to his fellow scientists, none would so little "glorify at the mysteriousness of Nature" or so much dare "experiment intelligently" as he.
text:
"Was man an der Natur Geheimnisvolles pries, Das wagen wir verständig zu probiren, Und was sie sonst organisiren liess, Das lassen wir Krystallisiren."
In the scale of his affections stand in order, Chemistry, biology, and the ladies. Although he would use the first person plural "wir" out of deference to his fellow scientists, none would so little "glorify at the mysteriousness of Nature" or so much dare "experiment intelligently" as he.

name: Grissom, Bennie R.
motto: "Smooth runs the water where the brook is deep."
description: Quiet, placid, reserved, unostentatious; yet, like the brook, when the leap is to be made, does it unhesitatingly, unerringly, and again runs smooth. Large thoughts behind his quiet mien, and large possibilities masked by his sober, thoughtful demeanor. One whom to know is to either respect or to like, often both, always the one.
text:
"Smooth runs the water where the brook is deep."
Quiet, placid, reserved, unostentatious; yet, like the brook, when the leap is to be made, does it unhesitatingly, unerringly, and again runs smooth. Large thoughts behind his quiet mien, and large possibilities masked by his sober, thoughtful demeanor. One whom to know is to either respect or to like, often both, always the one.

name: Guess, Richard Malcolm
motto: "Our echoes roll from soul to soul."
description: It is to be wished that all of us had in the course of our college days started as many clear and inspiring echoes on their way as has Malcolm. He has spent four or five years giving us a working idea of what a man ought to be, and every fellow will agree who has talked to him and felt his influence like a cold, wholesome wind blowing off the snowy heights of a spotless character, driving before it the mists of doubt, and spurring us to vigorous upward efforts.
text:
"Our echoes roll from soul to soul."
It is to be wished that all of us had in the course of our college days started as many clear and inspiring echoes on their way as has Malcolm. He has spent four or five years giving us a working idea of what a man ought to be, and every fellow will agree who has talked to him and felt his influence like a cold, wholesome wind blowing off the snowy heights of a spotless character, driving before it the mists of doubt, and spurring us to vigorous upward efforts.

name: Haralson, M. Flint
motto: "I dare do all that may become a man."
description: Flint just had to make all those teams so that he could do some sparking on the trips. He is a good student and has countless friends, as the county papers say. Also, he never forgets his duty to the Freshmen. Tied World's record on High Hurdles.
text:
"I dare do all that may become a man."
Flint just had to make all those teams so that he could do some sparking on the trips. He is a good student and has countless friends, as the county papers say. Also, he never forgets his duty to the Freshmen. Tied World's record on High Hurdles.

name: Haxton, Richard Kenneth
motto: "Le Chevalier sans peur et sans reproche."
description: What shall we say of Lena that has not already been said by the dope sheets proclaiming his prowess on the football field,—by profs admiring a man who could be an athlete of the first rank and still satisfy scholastic requirements,—or by the boys who know him to be a friend whose mettle will stand the severest test? The fact that he was awarded the second largest number of votes for the best all-around man in school is better testimony to his excellencies than I know how to give otherwise.
text:
"Le Chevalier sans peur et sans reproche."
What shall we say of Lena that has not already been said by the dope sheets proclaiming his prowess on the football field,—by profs admiring a man who could be an athlete of the first rank and still satisfy scholastic requirements,—or by the boys who know him to be a friend whose mettle will stand the severest test? The fact that he was awarded the second largest number of votes for the best all-around man in school is better testimony to his excellencies than I know how to give otherwise.

name: Hickey, Pearl
motto: "Wearing all that weight of learning lightly like a flower."
description: We shall content ourselves by saying, in addition to the highly appropriate quotation, that she was awarded a very high place in the co-ed popularity contest, and that her place in her classes has likewise been with the very best.
text:
"Wearing all that weight of learning lightly like a flower."
We shall content ourselves by saying, in addition to the highly appropriate quotation, that she was awarded a very high place in the co-ed popularity contest, and that her place in her classes has likewise been with the very best.

name: Hill, David A.
motto: "No solemn sanctimonious face I pull."
description: Three years have proved plenty of time for David to satisfy the proper persons that he possessed the required "Great worth of wit," and his graduation in that time proves what a man can do with a good mind and close application.
text:
"No solemn sanctimonious face I pull."
Three years have proved plenty of time for David to satisfy the proper persons that he possessed the required "Great worth of wit," and his graduation in that time proves what a man can do with a good mind and close application.

name: Hudson, James Kyle
motto: "O, for a beaker full of the warm south!"
description: There are few of us who have not envied this versatile youth his ability to laugh away the night, and, from some unknown source, find material in his head for a splendid recitation the next morning. We do not know of any Sunday-school Class that is counting on him for their future instruction, but we are sure that if there were, his liberal supply of brains would insure their not being disappointed.
text:
"O, for a beaker full of the warm south!"
There are few of us who have not envied this versatile youth his ability to laugh away the night, and, from some unknown source, find material in his head for a splendid recitation the next morning. We do not know of any Sunday-school Class that is counting on him for their future instruction, but we are sure that if there were, his liberal supply of brains would insure their not being disappointed.

name: Hudson, Arthur Palmer
motto: "When the secret cup Of still and serious thoughts goes round, It seems as if he drinks it up He feels with spirit so profound."
description: Two years of the files of the largest college weekly in the South is a monument to one of the phases of Hudson's college work and it is a monument not to be despised. Hudson is our most intellectual man, in the highest and deepest sense of that flexible term, and we hope that his chosen profession of journalism will not wean him entirely away from literature pure and undefiled.
text:
"When the secret cup Of still and serious thoughts goes round, It seems as if he drinks it up He feels with spirit so profound."
Two years of the files of the largest college weekly in the South is a monument to one of the phases of Hudson's college work and it is a monument not to be despised. Hudson is our most intellectual man, in the highest and deepest sense of that flexible term, and we hope that his chosen profession of journalism will not wean him entirely away from literature pure and undefiled.

name: James, Jerome Parker
motto: "A good tall fellow."
description: Immediately after his arrival in our midst, James fell heir to the suggestive title of "Jesse James," but he has failed so far to show any such brigandish or bloodthirsty characteristics as his name would indicate that he possessed. However, we will not answer for what Uncle Tommie may drive him to next year.
text:
"A good tall fellow."
Immediately after his arrival in our midst, James fell heir to the suggestive title of "Jesse James," but he has failed so far to show any such brigandish or bloodthirsty characteristics as his name would indicate that he possessed. However, we will not answer for what Uncle Tommie may drive him to next year.

name: Jenkins, Francis Colbert
motto: "Sir, I lack advancement."
clubs: Phi Sigma Leader in Student Congress
description: He entered the University as a freshman who lacked advancement. He's been lacking it ever since, and if you will refer to above list of advancements, you will see that he has been getting it—advancement.
text:
"Sir, I lack advancement."
He entered the University as a freshman who lacked advancement. He's been lacking it ever since, and if you will refer to above list of advancements, you will see that he has been getting it—advancement.

name: Johnston, Henry Grady
motto: "His only crime,—That most resplendent hair."
description: We must forgive Red his hair, though, for he is the merriest, kindest soul that ever danced a measure. A little given to tales of the marvellous as happening in Hernando, he is yet pardonable on the grounds that he knows that nobody believes him. This sketch must be too brief to do justice to Red's many virtues and attractions, but we know that he will take it as he takes everything that is kindly meant,—with a laugh.
text:
"His only crime,—That most resplendent hair."
We must forgive Red his hair, though, for he is the merriest, kindest soul that ever danced a measure. A little given to tales of the marvellous as happening in Hernando, he is yet pardonable on the grounds that he knows that nobody believes him. This sketch must be too brief to do justice to Red's many virtues and attractions, but we know that he will take it as he takes everything that is kindly meant,—with a laugh.

name: Kellis, Patty Pleas
motto: "Who mixed reason with pleasure and wisdom with mirth. If he had any faults, he has left us in doubt."
description: We hear that Patty expects to be a farmer. If he is as congenial with the "lowing kine" and the other denizens of the farm-yard as he has been with us with whom he has come into contact at school, we predict that he will be another boost for agricultural prosperity.
text:
"Who mixed reason with pleasure and wisdom with mirth. If he had any faults, he has left us in doubt."
We hear that Patty expects to be a farmer. If he is as congenial with the "lowing kine" and the other denizens of the farm-yard as he has been with us with whom he has come into contact at school, we predict that he will be another boost for agricultural prosperity.

name: Kent, C. M.
motto: "His heart as far from fraud as heaven from earth."
description: Kilmichael is his town, a very suggestive name for the home of a prospective doctor, but perhaps he will not turn out quite as bad as that. He looks to the "Lone Star State" to furnish him the other half of his dual existence.
text:
"His heart as far from fraud as heaven from earth."
Kilmichael is his town, a very suggestive name for the home of a prospective doctor, but perhaps he will not turn out quite as bad as that. He looks to the "Lone Star State" to furnish him the other half of his dual existence.

name: Livingston, Edward Martin
motto: "Still to be neat,—still to be drest As you were going to a feast."
description: It is a mark of his good taste in other things beside dress that though he started his college education elsewhere, yet he soon came round to reason, and for the last two years has been with us here. One of Uncle Tommy's next year minions.
text:
"Still to be neat,—still to be drest As you were going to a feast."
It is a mark of his good taste in other things beside dress that though he started his college education elsewhere, yet he soon came round to reason, and for the last two years has been with us here. One of Uncle Tommy's next year minions.

name: McClatchey, George Gray
motto: "The unco canny Scot."
description: "Mac" never boasted of it—he's too canny. He's one of those fellows endowed with a nature which thrives on much solitude and strict attention to personal business.
text:
"The unco canny Scot."
"Mac" never boasted of it—he's too canny. He's one of those fellows endowed with a nature which thrives on much solitude and strict attention to personal business.

name: McKie, Alva Burton
motto: "Whence is thy learning? Doth thy toil O'er pond'rous books consumed the midnight oil?"
description: Burt has written up all the meds., and has left us very few complimentary things that one can conscientiously say about a medico. However we can say that he will make a model family doctor. Methinks I see him now,—patting a puling infant on the head while he pours a nauseous dose down its shrieking mouth.
text:
"Whence is thy learning? Doth thy toil O'er pond'rous books consumed the midnight oil?"
Burt has written up all the meds., and has left us very few complimentary things that one can conscientiously say about a medico. However we can say that he will make a model family doctor. Methinks I see him now,—patting a puling infant on the head while he pours a nauseous dose down its shrieking mouth.

name: McLeod, J. Angus
motto: "Some Cupid kills with arrows, some with traps."
description: Our class lover, self-nominated, but elected by acclamation. On any spring morning last year "Anguish" might have been seen waiting with eagerness for the descent of his deity, that he might take on him the pleasant burden of her school-books. "All the world loves a lover."
text:
"Some Cupid kills with arrows, some with traps."
Our class lover, self-nominated, but elected by acclamation. On any spring morning last year "Anguish" might have been seen waiting with eagerness for the descent of his deity, that he might take on him the pleasant burden of her school-books. "All the world loves a lover."

name: Autrey, William Mangum
motto: "Winding up days with toil and nights with sleep."
description: Industrious, purposeful, resourceful. He has within him the stuff of eternal youth, and can appreciate a joke of his own design or sufferance.
text:
"Winding up days with toil and nights with sleep."
Industrious, purposeful, resourceful. He has within him the stuff of eternal youth, and can appreciate a joke of his own design or sufferance.

name: May, L. P.
motto: "Virtue is like a rich stone,—best when plain set."
description: Though he has carried about as much work as anybody in school, May has never been so taken up with it that he didn't have time for a joke and a friendly chat. A pedagogue of great promise, if a thorough knowledge of a good part of what is offered here be any recommendation.
text:
"Virtue is like a rich stone,—best when plain set."
Though he has carried about as much work as anybody in school, May has never been so taken up with it that he didn't have time for a joke and a friendly chat. A pedagogue of great promise, if a thorough knowledge of a good part of what is offered here be any recommendation.

name: Mayo, Thomas Franklin
motto: "To be a well favored man is a gift of fortune, But to write and read comes by nature."
clubs: Y. M. C. A. Cabinet, 1911-'12 and 1912-'13
description: Now, it wouldn't be fair to hold Tom responsible for his face and er-er-head. They were not his work—'twas the gift of fortune. But to his credit ascribe the rich development of his inner self. His stories and other writings have pleased us all. He is distinctly literary, having a taste for and fine appreciation of good books. Along with this, he is full of fun—always ready to have a brush applied to him or to aid in the application thereof to another. Capable, efficient and lovable, he's a splendid combination.
text:
"To be a well favored man is a gift of fortune, But to write and read comes by nature."
Now, it wouldn't be fair to hold Tom responsible for his face and er-er-head. They were not his work—'twas the gift of fortune. But to his credit ascribe the rich development of his inner self. His stories and other writings have pleased us all. He is distinctly literary, having a taste for and fine appreciation of good books. Along with this, he is full of fun—always ready to have a brush applied to him or to aid in the application thereof to another. Capable, efficient and lovable, he's a splendid combination.

name: Miller, Warren Alford
motto: "My only books were woman's looks, And folly's all they've taught me."
description: With reference to the above quotation, we may say that "Dick" has a great many things that don't come out of books, not the least of which is a good-fellowship so irresistible that he goes out from us a favorite with boys and girls alike. We have no fears for the future prosperity of one who makes friends as fast as he makes acquaintances.
text:
"My only books were woman's looks, And folly's all they've taught me."
With reference to the above quotation, we may say that "Dick" has a great many things that don't come out of books, not the least of which is a good-fellowship so irresistible that he goes out from us a favorite with boys and girls alike. We have no fears for the future prosperity of one who makes friends as fast as he makes acquaintances.

name: Mitchell, Charles B.
motto: "Cheerfulness and content are great beautifiers, and are famous preservers of good looks."
description: The smiles and graces of the ladies cannot lure him from the calm and peaceful atmosphere, free from giggles, which he has chosen for himself,—this will be remedied in the course of time. He takes life as it comes with a smile.
text:
"Cheerfulness and content are great beautifiers, and are famous preservers of good looks."
The smiles and graces of the ladies cannot lure him from the calm and peaceful atmosphere, free from giggles, which he has chosen for himself,—this will be remedied in the course of time. He takes life as it comes with a smile.

name: Pannell, J. M.
motto: "If honor gives greatness, he's great as a king."
description: One of our considerable cohort of Benedicks, whose disposition seems to be entirely unspoiled by the trials of that felicitous state.
text:
"If honor gives greatness, he's great as a king."
One of our considerable cohort of Benedicks, whose disposition seems to be entirely unspoiled by the trials of that felicitous state.

name: Pound, Robert Earl
motto: "The tall, the wise head."
description: One of the first things a Freshman sees that he thinks is worthy of notice at the University, is Pap's dancing. Perhaps the same thing that makes him a good dancer makes him a good basket ball player,—at any rate he is one of the best we have. If he turns out to be as good at cutting people as he is at cutting a step, his fortune as a surgeon is made.
text:
"The tall, the wise head."
One of the first things a Freshman sees that he thinks is worthy of notice at the University, is Pap's dancing. Perhaps the same thing that makes him a good dancer makes him a good basket ball player,—at any rate he is one of the best we have. If he turns out to be as good at cutting people as he is at cutting a step, his fortune as a surgeon is made.

name: Ramsay, Alfred Hearne
motto: "Motley's the only wear."
description: In a better age than this he might have fairly rivaled Touchstone. No situation so serious or trivial that he does not find an applicable joke, anecdote or yarn. Wherefore, he is known to his fellow as "Remus," that southern counterpart of Touchstone's ilk.
text:
"Motley's the only wear."
In a better age than this he might have fairly rivaled Touchstone. No situation so serious or trivial that he does not find an applicable joke, anecdote or yarn. Wherefore, he is known to his fellow as "Remus," that southern counterpart of Touchstone's ilk.

name: Roberson, William
motto: "Bid me discourse—I will enchant thine ear."
description: Many and fair are the years that he has enchanted, and he must have tried to advantage some of the same chin-music on the profs, for his graduation year is only his third. He enjoys the reputation of being the only man in school who is so popular that his visitors shoot off his lock when they are denied entrance.
text:
"Bid me discourse—I will enchant thine ear."
Many and fair are the years that he has enchanted, and he must have tried to advantage some of the same chin-music on the profs, for his graduation year is only his third. He enjoys the reputation of being the only man in school who is so popular that his visitors shoot off his lock when they are denied entrance.

name: Rogers, James Fant
motto: "So dost thou travel on life's common way In cheerful godliness."
description: The kind of man for a fellow to be advised by, if he wants good sense from a clear head, only Fant has certain untrustworthy opinions about Bryant's poetry that Professor Bishop does not wholly endorse. If anyone of us happens to have any money he would do well to deposit it in that bank of his. In the searching light of a long, close friendship, as clean, as strong, and withal as warm-hearted and jolly a man as we have met.
text:
"So dost thou travel on life's common way In cheerful godliness."
The kind of man for a fellow to be advised by, if he wants good sense from a clear head, only Fant has certain untrustworthy opinions about Bryant's poetry that Professor Bishop does not wholly endorse. If anyone of us happens to have any money he would do well to deposit it in that bank of his. In the searching light of a long, close friendship, as clean, as strong, and withal as warm-hearted and jolly a man as we have met.

name: Simmons, J. Douglas, Jr.
motto: "I hate nobody,—I am in charity with the world."
description: The only thing he is niggardly with is his frown. He has few faults beside coming from Pontotoc. Dug expects to set up an undertaking establishment in connection with his medical practice.
text:
"I hate nobody,—I am in charity with the world."
The only thing he is niggardly with is his frown. He has few faults beside coming from Pontotoc. Dug expects to set up an undertaking establishment in connection with his medical practice.

name: Stone, Phil
motto: "Life is not so short, but there is always time for courtesy."
description: Our courtly gentleman from Oxford town has never been seen in any situation or condition however trying, in which he failed to display the most beautiful of manners. Nor, in this case, is manners all the man, for the University records show four years of brilliant grades for Phil, and he has likewise found time to leave his mark among the boys and in society.
text:
"Life is not so short, but there is always time for courtesy."
Our courtly gentleman from Oxford town has never been seen in any situation or condition however trying, in which he failed to display the most beautiful of manners. Nor, in this case, is manners all the man, for the University records show four years of brilliant grades for Phil, and he has likewise found time to leave his mark among the boys and in society.

name: Stone, John Pittman
motto: "A merrier man Within the limits of becoming mirth, I never spent an hour's talk withal."
description: This was one senior for whom it was very easy to find a fitting quotation, for there is an abundance of poetry written in praise of the jovial god of good-fellowship. His is one of those merry faces which will smile in ghostly fashion from the walls of Gordon Hall on the revellers of the future if, by chance, they drink a toast to the best of the fellows that came before them.
text:
"A merrier man Within the limits of becoming mirth, I never spent an hour's talk withal."
This was one senior for whom it was very easy to find a fitting quotation, for there is an abundance of poetry written in praise of the jovial god of good-fellowship. His is one of those merry faces which will smile in ghostly fashion from the walls of Gordon Hall on the revellers of the future if, by chance, they drink a toast to the best of the fellows that came before them.

name: Sutherland, Hugh Lewis, Jr.
motto: "Between two girls, which hath the merriest eye, I have perhaps some goodly spirit of judgment."
description: Though the quotation may seem to indicate that "Nig" is a great ladies' man, yet he is none the less liked by the boys than by the girls; he is one of the lawyer band, and has already settled down to Blackstone and the midnight oil.
text:
"Between two girls, which hath the merriest eye, I have perhaps some goodly spirit of judgment."
Though the quotation may seem to indicate that "Nig" is a great ladies' man, yet he is none the less liked by the boys than by the girls; he is one of the lawyer band, and has already settled down to Blackstone and the midnight oil.

name: Taylor, Mildred
motto: "When you do dance, I wish you a wave o' the sea, that you might ever do nothing but that."
description: Not the least lovely of a group of co-eds who have helped make college life pleasant. Gracious to everybody, always up on her lessons, she is as much at home in the private life of the Romans as at a Junior Prom Ball,—in playing for the Y. M. C. A., as in leading a train of doting youths about the campus. Such as she are the best justification of co-education.
text:
"When you do dance, I wish you a wave o' the sea, that you might ever do nothing but that."
Not the least lovely of a group of co-eds who have helped make college life pleasant. Gracious to everybody, always up on her lessons, she is as much at home in the private life of the Romans as at a Junior Prom Ball,—in playing for the Y. M. C. A., as in leading a train of doting youths about the campus. Such as she are the best justification of co-education.

name: Turner, George W.
motto: "Linked sweetness long drawn out."
description: There is so much to "Lengthy" that we cannot hope to do justice to all of him in the little space allotted us. So we will have to say only that he is just six feet three of good nature and of good heart.
text:
"Linked sweetness long drawn out."
There is so much to "Lengthy" that we cannot hope to do justice to all of him in the little space allotted us. So we will have to say only that he is just six feet three of good nature and of good heart.

name: White, Morris Edward
motto: "O, 'tis a parlous boy; Bold, quick, ingenious, forward, capable."
clubs: Treasurer of Y. M. C. A., 1912-'13
Business Manager of U. of M. Magazine, 1910-'11
M. I. O. A. Representative Tatise, 1913
description: Nature was generous with precious materials when she poured into her alembic the elements from which she was to distill his character and personality. She put therein the qualities from which the adjectives in the quotation are derived.
text:
"O, 'tis a parlous boy; Bold, quick, ingenious, forward, capable."
Nature was generous with precious materials when she poured into her alembic the elements from which she was to distill his character and personality. She put therein the qualities from which the adjectives in the quotation are derived.

name: Young, John W., Jr.
motto: "We can say nothing but what hath been said—He that comes last is commonly best."
description: OLE MISS 1913. The boy with the booming basso will be missed in all places where music is loved, whether in church choir or in midnight quartette. Of late, he has been sorely afflicted by Dan Cupid, but as he says that "She" is now "My-Own," we suppose matters have taken a favorable turn, and that "Country" will put on flesh again.
text:
"We can say nothing but what hath been said—He that comes last is commonly best."
OLE MISS 1913. The boy with the booming basso will be missed in all places where music is loved, whether in church choir or in midnight quartette. Of late, he has been sorely afflicted by Dan Cupid, but as he says that "She" is now "My-Own," we suppose matters have taken a favorable turn, and that "Country" will put on flesh again.

name: Nelson, David G.
motto: "Like the driving of Jehu the son of Nimshi, for he driveth furiously."
description: OLE MISS 1913. David and his ever-hospitable car are the co-eds' delight, but David without the car would have the same heart of gold and pleasant frankness of manner that makes us like him now. His head is no whit sunnier than his disposition, nor his freckles more numerous than his good traits.
text:
"Like the driving of Jehu the son of Nimshi, for he driveth furiously."
OLE MISS 1913. David and his ever-hospitable car are the co-eds' delight, but David without the car would have the same heart of gold and pleasant frankness of manner that makes us like him now. His head is no whit sunnier than his disposition, nor his freckles more numerous than his good traits.

name: Borchert, Alice
motto: "A kind and gentle heart she had."
description: OLE MISS 1913. We envy the school children who fall heir to such a teacher as she is bound to be, for everybody at the Hall knows how patiently she puts up with all the persecutions that the roving disposition of her next door neighbors subject her to. Anyone who can have her hair pins stolen and still keep a bright outlook on life is fit to cope with the toughest problems.
text:
"A kind and gentle heart she had."
OLE MISS 1913. We envy the school children who fall heir to such a teacher as she is bound to be, for everybody at the Hall knows how patiently she puts up with all the persecutions that the roving disposition of her next door neighbors subject her to. Anyone who can have her hair pins stolen and still keep a bright outlook on life is fit to cope with the toughest problems.

name: Lee, Muna
motto: "Fraught with learning."
description: OLE MISS 1913. Miss Lee has been with us only this one year, but all who have had classes with her have found that she is a person with brains. We are glad that she came to us in time for Ole Miss to claim her as one of her daughters.
text:
"Fraught with learning."
OLE MISS 1913. Miss Lee has been with us only this one year, but all who have had classes with her have found that she is a person with brains. We are glad that she came to us in time for Ole Miss to claim her as one of her daughters.

name: Seymour, Edwin N.
description: OLE MISS 1913.
text: OLE MISS 1913.

name: Boggan, J. M.
motto: "Lay on McDuff, And damned be he who first cries, 'Hold, enough!'"
description: When John gets into a thing, he's in for all there is to it. While not over-ready to "lay on," he's pretty good at the laying on business when such is the order. John is one of those fellows who saws wood and says little. He looks out for his own affairs and little interferes with others.
text:
"Lay on McDuff, And damned be he who first cries, 'Hold, enough!'"
When John gets into a thing, he's in for all there is to it. While not over-ready to "lay on," he's pretty good at the laying on business when such is the order. John is one of those fellows who saws wood and says little. He looks out for his own affairs and little interferes with others.

name: Byrnes, C. F.
motto: "Zounds! I was never so bethump'd with words Since first I called my brother's father dad."
description: Bobby does use a lot of words. But, do you know, it's because he thinks faster than he can express himself. Still, he gets the "meat out of the coconut." If the thought is there, what does it matter if couched in numerous words? Man never had such an imagination as has he. He has a multitude of figures at his tongue's end which give worlds of humor to some of his descriptions.
text:
"Zounds! I was never so bethump'd with words Since first I called my brother's father dad."
Bobby does use a lot of words. But, do you know, it's because he thinks faster than he can express himself. Still, he gets the "meat out of the coconut." If the thought is there, what does it matter if couched in numerous words? Man never had such an imagination as has he. He has a multitude of figures at his tongue's end which give worlds of humor to some of his descriptions.

name: Cohn, H. L.
motto: "He laughed and laughed, as though it were fun to laugh."
description: Now here's a big hearted, warm natured, jovial fellow. He reminds one of a day in summer—all sunny and happy—you know. Heinie seldom permits care to sit upon his Herculean shoulders. He tells a joke well and has a store of them to tell. Here's material for a politician, not the modern day kind—the subtle manipulator—but the kind that people vote for because they like them.
text:
"He laughed and laughed, as though it were fun to laugh."
Now here's a big hearted, warm natured, jovial fellow. He reminds one of a day in summer—all sunny and happy—you know. Heinie seldom permits care to sit upon his Herculean shoulders. He tells a joke well and has a store of them to tell. Here's material for a politician, not the modern day kind—the subtle manipulator—but the kind that people vote for because they like them.

name: Coleman, E. F.
motto: "Ah! why should life all labor be?"
description: Coleman gets there all right, but he is not over fond of the strenuous. A pipe and warm fire to dream over are much more to his liking. He likes the easy, happy-go-lucky. However, he does his work and attends to his own business, which, after all, is a pretty good thing for a man to do.
text:
"Ah! why should life all labor be?"
Coleman gets there all right, but he is not over fond of the strenuous. A pipe and warm fire to dream over are much more to his liking. He likes the easy, happy-go-lucky. However, he does his work and attends to his own business, which, after all, is a pretty good thing for a man to do.

name: Conner, C. E.
motto: "Men of few words are the best men."
description: Those who know him call him "Dutch." The origin of the name isn't known. He is seldom seen with the other fellows. It isn't his vocation to "swap yarns." But some of us have seen within his inner sanctuary. His blood is red and thick.
text:
"Men of few words are the best men."
Those who know him call him "Dutch." The origin of the name isn't known. He is seldom seen with the other fellows. It isn't his vocation to "swap yarns." But some of us have seen within his inner sanctuary. His blood is red and thick.

name: Day, I. W.
motto: "Talk of him to Jacob's ladder and he would ask the number of the steps."
description: There is no doubt in the world but that Day will be well informed. He permits nothing to escape his view, that is to say, if he doesn't understand, he asks for information. Some of us are too timid, or for other cause do not venture interrogatories—not so with Day. We cannot but admire his pluck. Few of us would attempt the law were we laboring under his disadvantages.
text:
"Talk of him to Jacob's ladder and he would ask the number of the steps."
There is no doubt in the world but that Day will be well informed. He permits nothing to escape his view, that is to say, if he doesn't understand, he asks for information. Some of us are too timid, or for other cause do not venture interrogatories—not so with Day. We cannot but admire his pluck. Few of us would attempt the law were we laboring under his disadvantages.

name: Foote, A. M.
motto: "The elements so mixed in him that nature might stand up and say, 'this is a man.'"
description: One of the questions of the day is, "What are they going to do without 'Bill' Foote next year?" There are few departments of school life where his energy, his good-fellowship, his common sense and patriotic enthusiasm for everything that helps the school will not be missed. Almost constantly in the public eye, "Feets" has never for a day lost the confidence and affection we all feel for him. To know him is to like him, and everybody knows him. We heartily agree with that fair lady and wise judge who pronounced him, "A real, real man."
text:
"The elements so mixed in him that nature might stand up and say, 'this is a man.'"
One of the questions of the day is, "What are they going to do without 'Bill' Foote next year?" There are few departments of school life where his energy, his good-fellowship, his common sense and patriotic enthusiasm for everything that helps the school will not be missed. Almost constantly in the public eye, "Feets" has never for a day lost the confidence and affection we all feel for him. To know him is to like him, and everybody knows him. We heartily agree with that fair lady and wise judge who pronounced him, "A real, real man."

name: Greene, W. G.
motto: "They say that men are moulded out of faults, And, for the most, become much more the better For being a little bad."
description: The poet who wrote the above had "Chick" in mind. He has his virtues though—bright, courageous and generous. Still, what man hasn't his faults? "Chick" has the human frailties—the innocent devilishness that makes one interesting.
text:
"They say that men are moulded out of faults, And, for the most, become much more the better For being a little bad."
The poet who wrote the above had "Chick" in mind. He has his virtues though—bright, courageous and generous. Still, what man hasn't his faults? "Chick" has the human frailties—the innocent devilishness that makes one interesting.

name: Kyle, J. W.
motto: "Born for Success he seemed, With grace to win, with heart to hold, With shining gifts that took all eyes."
major: B.A., '12; Literary and law degrees from the University and a course at Oxford, England
description: This man will enter the practice of law as well equipped as any, and better than most. But add to this his natural attitude, his brilliancy of mind and capacity for work, and you have a winner. Senator, we shall watch your progress with interest and well wishing.
text:
"Born for Success he seemed, With grace to win, with heart to hold, With shining gifts that took all eyes."
This man will enter the practice of law as well equipped as any, and better than most. But add to this his natural attitude, his brilliancy of mind and capacity for work, and you have a winner. Senator, we shall watch your progress with interest and well wishing.

name: Loch, Jno. W.
motto: "I am in earnest; I will not equivocate; I will not excuse; I will not retreat a Single inch; and I will be heard."
description: Nothing could describe him better. He's as accurate and punctilious as the sun as it swings through its orbit; uncompromising as a Scotch Laird; and fearless in his undertakings as Julius Caesar. Probably no man in the class works more assiduously than Loch.
text:
"I am in earnest; I will not equivocate; I will not excuse; I will not retreat a Single inch; and I will be heard."
Nothing could describe him better. He's as accurate and punctilious as the sun as it swings through its orbit; uncompromising as a Scotch Laird; and fearless in his undertakings as Julius Caesar. Probably no man in the class works more assiduously than Loch.

name: Mitchell, S. F.
motto: "A friend may well be reckoned a master-piece of nature."
description: Few who know Steve fail to like him—nay, love him. He's one of those fellows who just gets next to you. Self-sacrificing to a degree, he hesitates not the least to do kindness to others. But above all, we appreciate him for his big nature, his warm-hearted sincerity and rugged honesty. There are not many capable of friendship—here's one.
text:
"A friend may well be reckoned a master-piece of nature."
Few who know Steve fail to like him—nay, love him. He's one of those fellows who just gets next to you. Self-sacrificing to a degree, he hesitates not the least to do kindness to others. But above all, we appreciate him for his big nature, his warm-hearted sincerity and rugged honesty. There are not many capable of friendship—here's one.

name: Montgomery, V. B.
motto: "He was a scholar, and a ripe and good one; Exceeding wise, fair spoke and persuading; Lofty and sour to them that loved him not; But to them that knew him, as sweet as summer."
description: He has the knack, the brains and will to study and consequently he does study, long and hard. His grade shows for his work, for he stands right at the head of his class.
text:
"He was a scholar, and a ripe and good one; Exceeding wise, fair spoke and persuading; Lofty and sour to them that loved him not; But to them that knew him, as sweet as summer."
He has the knack, the brains and will to study and consequently he does study, long and hard. His grade shows for his work, for he stands right at the head of his class.

name: Oates, O. M.
motto: "And many strokes, though with a little axe, Hew down and fell the hardest timbered oak."
description: When it comes to application, Oates is the "only and original." He's a regular apple for application—just applies and applies. Oates is the kind that gets there, even though they have to work a little to do it. It isn't that his axe is so small but he has the multiplicity of strokes, large or small the axe, and he'll cut the tree, see if he doesn't. Whoa! Baby! Whoa! Babe!
text:
"And many strokes, though with a little axe, Hew down and fell the hardest timbered oak."
When it comes to application, Oates is the "only and original." He's a regular apple for application—just applies and applies. Oates is the kind that gets there, even though they have to work a little to do it. It isn't that his axe is so small but he has the multiplicity of strokes, large or small the axe, and he'll cut the tree, see if he doesn't. Whoa! Baby! Whoa! Babe!

name: Patrick, W. J.
motto: "Talks as familiarly of roaring lions As maids of thirteen do of puppy-dogs."
description: Pat is Irish to the core. Nothing to him is impossible. Did you ever notice the confidence and optimism in the Irish? Well, Pat has every bit of it, which, coupled with another Irish trait, a sense of humor, will go a long way toward his success in life. He has the straightforward directness and simple honesty, rare to-day, that men seize upon when offered.
text:
"Talks as familiarly of roaring lions As maids of thirteen do of puppy-dogs."
Pat is Irish to the core. Nothing to him is impossible. Did you ever notice the confidence and optimism in the Irish? Well, Pat has every bit of it, which, coupled with another Irish trait, a sense of humor, will go a long way toward his success in life. He has the straightforward directness and simple honesty, rare to-day, that men seize upon when offered.

name: Rucker, J. D.
motto: "It can be said of him, when he departs, he took a Man's life with him."
description: Sober, wise and just, from this fellow we may expect much. He carries his accomplishments as modestly as a maid; performs his duty as becomes a man—in all his dealings fair and conscientious. A sound discretion and strong intellect supported by an earnest and upright character, assures for him high attainment.
text:
"It can be said of him, when he departs, he took a Man's life with him."
Sober, wise and just, from this fellow we may expect much. He carries his accomplishments as modestly as a maid; performs his duty as becomes a man—in all his dealings fair and conscientious. A sound discretion and strong intellect supported by an earnest and upright character, assures for him high attainment.

name: Warren, H. E.
motto: "Ham. Do you see yonder cloud that's almost in shape of camel? Pol. By the mass, and 'tis like a camel, indeed. Ham. Methinks 'tis like a weasel? Pol. 'tis backed like a weasel. Ham. Or like a whale? Pol. Very, Very like a whale."
description: The most non-committal fellow in captivity. Absolutely opposed to positive statement or definite assertion, he employs provisions, suppositions and conditions without limitation. But with all that, Warren is with you when he thinks you're right.
text:
"Ham. Do you see yonder cloud that's almost in shape of camel? Pol. By the mass, and 'tis like a camel, indeed. Ham. Methinks 'tis like a weasel? Pol. 'tis backed like a weasel. Ham. Or like a whale? Pol. Very, Very like a whale."
The most non-committal fellow in captivity. Absolutely opposed to positive statement or definite assertion, he employs provisions, suppositions and conditions without limitation. But with all that, Warren is with you when he thinks you're right.

name: Wilroy, N. E.
motto: "And wrinkles, the damned democrats, won't flatter."
major: U of M, Literary degree, Law
description: So far as good looks go, they may not flatter, still, doubtless they indicate traits of the inner nature which compliment most loudly. Here's a man rich in experience, fresh from fields to most of us unknown. He took his literary degree sometime back in the mediaeval period, "When knights, etc., etc.," and now he is to get a law "dip." His fifteen years at the front should be an invaluable aid to him in his early practice.
text:
"And wrinkles, the damned democrats, won't flatter."
So far as good looks go, they may not flatter, still, doubtless they indicate traits of the inner nature which compliment most loudly. Here's a man rich in experience, fresh from fields to most of us unknown. He took his literary degree sometime back in the mediaeval period, "When knights, etc., etc.," and now he is to get a law "dip." His fifteen years at the front should be an invaluable aid to him in his early practice.

name: Wilson, T. W.
motto: "But if it be a sin to covet honors, I am the most offending soul alive."
major: U of M, L. L. B. '13
clubs: Selected M. I. O. A. representative '12
description: "Pedro" has almost created a corner in the honor market. Look at the formidable array opposite his name and judge of his, "Offending soul." Be it said though, he deserves them each and all. Mark our prediction, Mississippi will ere long boast another great criminal lawyer.
text:
"But if it be a sin to covet honors, I am the most offending soul alive."
"Pedro" has almost created a corner in the honor market. Look at the formidable array opposite his name and judge of his, "Offending soul." Be it said though, he deserves them each and all. Mark our prediction, Mississippi will ere long boast another great criminal lawyer.

name: Farish, J. W.
motto: "If you want to run a line to heaven or sink a shaft to hell."
description: That we see very little of John on the campus and up town is a pretty good evidence of his studiousness, even if he didn't have that Taylor medal to prove this fact by. He is indeed of that type who work while his companions sleep, and he is sure to reap the reward that the poet promises such youths.
text:
"If you want to run a line to heaven or sink a shaft to hell."
That we see very little of John on the campus and up town is a pretty good evidence of his studiousness, even if he didn't have that Taylor medal to prove this fact by. He is indeed of that type who work while his companions sleep, and he is sure to reap the reward that the poet promises such youths.

name: Limerick, Richard Connor
motto: "For he by geometric scale Can take the size of pots of ale."
clubs: Y. M. C. A. Cabinet, '11 and '12
description: The pots of ale mentioned above are not his own, however, for he is not only a remarkable mathematician, but as steady as a die, and one of the most thoroughly dependable men in school. A man who has fought "little Allie" for four years with glory, surely has something great in store for him.
text:
"For he by geometric scale Can take the size of pots of ale."
The pots of ale mentioned above are not his own, however, for he is not only a remarkable mathematician, but as steady as a die, and one of the most thoroughly dependable men in school. A man who has fought "little Allie" for four years with glory, surely has something great in store for him.

name: Seymour, Edwin Newburger
motto: "Order is heaven's first law."
major: B. S.; B. E. Civil Engineering
description: Our present victim conforms conspicuously to the heavenly law mentioned above, in his immaculate dressing and in the care which has rendered vain all Little Allie's worst wiles and deepest-laid snares. We have no doubt that Edwin has already "figured" the way to success, to a fine point; we can only hope that it will be a flowery one.
text:
"Order is heaven's first law."
Our present victim conforms conspicuously to the heavenly law mentioned above, in his immaculate dressing and in the care which has rendered vain all Little Allie's worst wiles and deepest-laid snares. We have no doubt that Edwin has already "figured" the way to success, to a fine point; we can only hope that it will be a flowery one.

name: Stall, E. B.
motto: "The story of a brave engineer."
description: He expects to find enough engineering work to do in paving the streets of Oxford, and we are sure that succeeding generations will rise up and call him blessed if he succeeds.
text:
"The story of a brave engineer."
He expects to find enough engineering work to do in paving the streets of Oxford, and we are sure that succeeding generations will rise up and call him blessed if he succeeds.

name: Morris, J. Alexander, Jr.
motto: "And fearless minds climb soonest unto crowns."
description: Alex landed here from C. H. A., but is none the worse for the wear. He is willing to try anything once, for he is usually first to take the anatomy practicals, and this is true bravery. His favorite musical instrument is the dinner-bell, and he is present at all its recitals.
text:
"And fearless minds climb soonest unto crowns."
Alex landed here from C. H. A., but is none the worse for the wear. He is willing to try anything once, for he is usually first to take the anatomy practicals, and this is true bravery. His favorite musical instrument is the dinner-bell, and he is present at all its recitals.

name: Reuben, A. Barker
motto: "A faultless body and a blameless mind."
description: Rube is a quiet sort of cuss and if he would only study a little more would be at the head of his class. He is a great athlete, but the toughest football play is but a dream, he says, compared to bucking the line of anatomical relations.
text:
"A faultless body and a blameless mind."
Rube is a quiet sort of cuss and if he would only study a little more would be at the head of his class. He is a great athlete, but the toughest football play is but a dream, he says, compared to bucking the line of anatomical relations.

name: Thomas, T. Batson
motto: "He hath a daily beauty in his life that makes me ugly."
description: The mourning, suffering, writhing, wailing sick of Hattiesburg, R. F. D. await with confidence his skill to free them from their demons. "Gee, if I can only get by this term they'll never catch me in this fix again."
text:
"He hath a daily beauty in his life that makes me ugly."
The mourning, suffering, writhing, wailing sick of Hattiesburg, R. F. D. await with confidence his skill to free them from their demons. "Gee, if I can only get by this term they'll never catch me in this fix again."

name: Paul, Zollicoffer Brown
motto: "And when a lady's in the case, You know, all other things give place."
description: P. Z., sometimes seen with Alexander, is a practical hygienist and an all-around student. His voice, which may be likened unto that of the Tennessee mocking-bird, entrances all who come to chapel and hear it floating from the choir.
text:
"And when a lady's in the case, You know, all other things give place."
P. Z., sometimes seen with Alexander, is a practical hygienist and an all-around student. His voice, which may be likened unto that of the Tennessee mocking-bird, entrances all who come to chapel and hear it floating from the choir.

name: Childers, J. E.
motto: "I am as sober as a judge."
description: He is a chromogenic bacillus of a most jolly nature, though the haziness of anatomical facts often casts him into the depths of an impenetrable gloom, whence he finally emerges, however, all the brighter for the struggle.
text:
"I am as sober as a judge."
He is a chromogenic bacillus of a most jolly nature, though the haziness of anatomical facts often casts him into the depths of an impenetrable gloom, whence he finally emerges, however, all the brighter for the struggle.

name: Greaves, Peyton R.
motto: "Happy am I; from care I'm free! Why aren't they all contented like me?"
description: The name of his native town should be no slur on his character, for we have it from a reputable source that none of the inmates will associate with him. His conversation is the clearly crystallized precipitation of all that is most precious in the ferment of impression after the impertinent and obtrusive particles have evaporated from the memory.
text:
"Happy am I; from care I'm free! Why aren't they all contented like me?"
The name of his native town should be no slur on his character, for we have it from a reputable source that none of the inmates will associate with him. His conversation is the clearly crystallized precipitation of all that is most precious in the ferment of impression after the impertinent and obtrusive particles have evaporated from the memory.

name: Greene, Douglas G.
motto: "Of manners gentle, of affections mild, In wit a man, simplicity a child."
description: We won our first victory over Vandy when we got Dug away from her allegiance. Always has a joke on hand that is new to him, anyway.
text:
"Of manners gentle, of affections mild, In wit a man, simplicity a child."
We won our first victory over Vandy when we got Dug away from her allegiance. Always has a joke on hand that is new to him, anyway.

name: Maxwell, Victor W.
motto: "Studious of ease, and fond of humble things."
description: Grand, gloomy and peculiar, but not wrapped in the solitude of his own originality or of anything else. He has too many friends for that. And the same qualities that make every man that knows him like and trust him will make him a medical success.
text:
"Studious of ease, and fond of humble things."
Grand, gloomy and peculiar, but not wrapped in the solitude of his own originality or of anything else. He has too many friends for that. And the same qualities that make every man that knows him like and trust him will make him a medical success.

name: Rosenthal, Jacob
motto: "If the heart of a man is depressed with cares, The mist is dispelled when a woman appears."
description: Jake says that he used to laugh at the hard hit ones, but he don't laugh any more, for he is as hard hit as any of them,—not only hit, but hit by two at once. Surely no man had a more sweetly cruel fate. He is sentimentally disposed to harmony, but is originally incapable of a tune. He is still undecided between medicine and a hog farm.
text:
"If the heart of a man is depressed with cares, The mist is dispelled when a woman appears."
Jake says that he used to laugh at the hard hit ones, but he don't laugh any more, for he is as hard hit as any of them,—not only hit, but hit by two at once. Surely no man had a more sweetly cruel fate. He is sentimentally disposed to harmony, but is originally incapable of a tune. He is still undecided between medicine and a hog farm.

name: Francis, M. Tindall
motto: "I never knew so young a body with so old a head."
description: We may, without malice, say that he draws out the thread of his verbosity finer than the staple of his argument, but we like him no less for that. He seems to lean so towards law that we are sometimes fearful, that the medical profession will lose a good man.
text:
"I never knew so young a body with so old a head."
We may, without malice, say that he draws out the thread of his verbosity finer than the staple of his argument, but we like him no less for that. He seems to lean so towards law that we are sometimes fearful, that the medical profession will lose a good man.

name: Innaeus, Tucker
motto: "Give me a staff of honor for mine age, But not a sceptre to control the world."
description: What have I on my forceps now? Ah, 'tis Tuck! He is forever on the look-out for the ideal of his dreams. Athletics is his hobby, but eating his chief exercise. Because of, or in spite of all these things, he is going to come out on top.
text:
"Give me a staff of honor for mine age, But not a sceptre to control the world."
What have I on my forceps now? Ah, 'tis Tuck! He is forever on the look-out for the ideal of his dreams. Athletics is his hobby, but eating his chief exercise. Because of, or in spite of all these things, he is going to come out on top.

name: Willie, E. Vandevere
motto: "Cruel as death, and hungry as the grave."
description: Vandy, our pretty boy, is a cardiac pulverizer of pronounced ability, though he at last got his own heart caught in his own machine, and the lady carried it off to Okolona. He says it was a question of the farm or medicine, and he "took his medicine" like a man.
text:
"Cruel as death, and hungry as the grave."
Vandy, our pretty boy, is a cardiac pulverizer of pronounced ability, though he at last got his own heart caught in his own machine, and the lady carried it off to Okolona. He says it was a question of the farm or medicine, and he "took his medicine" like a man.

name: Eliam, Baxter Burns
motto: "I earn that I eat, get that I wear, owe no man hate, envy no man's happiness, glad of other man's good, content with my harm."
description: Ah, here he is! Redcliff is the burg we owe for this gift. He has his views on all subjects except Pathology. He puts the squirrels on the blink when it comes to laying away hickory nuts.
text:
"I earn that I eat, get that I wear, owe no man hate, envy no man's happiness, glad of other man's good, content with my harm."
Ah, here he is! Redcliff is the burg we owe for this gift. He has his views on all subjects except Pathology. He puts the squirrels on the blink when it comes to laying away hickory nuts.

name: Camp, Ernest L.
description: He did not derive his name from any personal traits of character. But he can be relied on to do what there is to be done in a satisfactory way. Being one of the best students in the class, we are assured of his success and the good wishes of us all go with him as we part.
text: He did not derive his name from any personal traits of character. But he can be relied on to do what there is to be done in a satisfactory way. Being one of the best students in the class, we are assured of his success and the good wishes of us all go with him as we part.

name: Carr, Felix O.
description: Felix, the "father of the class," good-natured and steady. He goes into life with the one aim: to do the best he can. No more can be expected from anyone.
text: Felix, the "father of the class," good-natured and steady. He goes into life with the one aim: to do the best he can. No more can be expected from anyone.

name: Cortright, E. G. B.
description: The chemist with the most original ideas ever demonstrated. He has not taken the interest in the class that he did last year, but this can be explained by the non-appearance of our lady-member this year. His perpetual smile will continue to win him friends as it has here.
text: The chemist with the most original ideas ever demonstrated. He has not taken the interest in the class that he did last year, but this can be explained by the non-appearance of our lady-member this year. His perpetual smile will continue to win him friends as it has here.

name: Duggins, Hugh Edward
description: The Pharmacist with a future. He has always manifested those unmistakable signs of a practical Dispenser of drugs. We expect to see him prosper in his profession.
text: The Pharmacist with a future. He has always manifested those unmistakable signs of a practical Dispenser of drugs. We expect to see him prosper in his profession.

name: Howe, William Norton
description: The "stoic" of the class is a native son. His regularity in everything has made him one to be counted on at all times. May the future bring him abundant success.
text: The "stoic" of the class is a native son. His regularity in everything has made him one to be counted on at all times. May the future bring him abundant success.

name: Martin, Roger McNeil
description: As he is known by the class, possesses the qualifications of a great "narrator" and as yet he has never met his equal in that line. He began his course here with the class of '12 but on account of over-work (?) was forced to rest the following session. His record gives him distinction in almost every branch of the course. Our prediction is that he will be a leader of "pill-rollers."
text: As he is known by the class, possesses the qualifications of a great "narrator" and as yet he has never met his equal in that line. He began his course here with the class of '12 but on account of over-work (?) was forced to rest the following session. His record gives him distinction in almost every branch of the course. Our prediction is that he will be a leader of "pill-rollers."

name: May, Clyde H.
description: Or anything else you want to call him is always on hand (except on Friday nights). Runt does the "society" for the class but he will have to hurry for he will claim the distinction of being the first "bald-headed" druggist in the class. Our wish is that his cares through life will be as few as through school.
text: Or anything else you want to call him is always on hand (except on Friday nights). Runt does the "society" for the class but he will have to hurry for he will claim the distinction of being the first "bald-headed" druggist in the class. Our wish is that his cares through life will be as few as through school.

name: Puller, John Shepard
description: Was willing to spend only one year at "Ole Miss" so he obtained his credits from Tulane, and thereby captures a "Dip" in one year. He avows that he will take an M. Ph. soon but we sometimes doubt this, in view of convincing indications that he will take a degree in matrimony sooner. We all feel assured of his success in the future.
text: Was willing to spend only one year at "Ole Miss" so he obtained his credits from Tulane, and thereby captures a "Dip" in one year. He avows that he will take an M. Ph. soon but we sometimes doubt this, in view of convincing indications that he will take a degree in matrimony sooner. We all feel assured of his success in the future.

name: Rowland, William Brewer
description: We often hear him mention Kosciusko in a very kindly tone, for what reason, we know not (?) He bids fair to make a success in the drug business as well as in matrimonial lines.
text: We often hear him mention Kosciusko in a very kindly tone, for what reason, we know not (?) He bids fair to make a success in the drug business as well as in matrimonial lines.

name: Stewart, Clifton Ardell
motto: "Give me Liberty or give me death."
description: If he is as persistent in the years to come as he has been here, we predict for him success.
text:
"Give me Liberty or give me death."
If he is as persistent in the years to come as he has been here, we predict for him success.

name: Tucker, A. S.
description: Tucker came to us from the bountiful Delta. He maintains a quiet unassuming attitude at all times, but notwithstanding this seemingly thoughtless mood he usually "gets by." We feel sure that he will enjoy the same success in the future as that in the past.
text: Tucker came to us from the bountiful Delta. He maintains a quiet unassuming attitude at all times, but notwithstanding this seemingly thoughtless mood he usually "gets by." We feel sure that he will enjoy the same success in the future as that in the past.

name: Woods, Walton T.
description: Very good-natured and the most innocent member of the Ph.G. bunch. He won his title of "Feathers" for brave deeds done in defense of the weaker sex. He is one of the brightest members of the class and is sure to make good on account of his fairness to everyone.
text: Very good-natured and the most innocent member of the Ph.G. bunch. He won his title of "Feathers" for brave deeds done in defense of the weaker sex. He is one of the brightest members of the class and is sure to make good on account of his fairness to everyone.

name: Yates, James Lewis
description: Yates came to us after spending one year at Valparaiso University; he was hard to be convinced that "Ole Miss" was the place to study pharmacy. Chemistry is his favorite study, as is shown by his good record in the different branches.
text: Yates came to us after spending one year at Valparaiso University; he was hard to be convinced that "Ole Miss" was the place to study pharmacy. Chemistry is his favorite study, as is shown by his good record in the different branches.

name: Collette, Allen
description: "Collie" drifted in here from Uncle Sam's Marine Hospital at New Orleans. He is well acquainted with the gridiron, having served two years on the Varsity. He is a very industrious fellow, and our confidence in him is shown by electing him to the Council of Honor.
text: "Collie" drifted in here from Uncle Sam's Marine Hospital at New Orleans. He is well acquainted with the gridiron, having served two years on the Varsity. He is a very industrious fellow, and our confidence in him is shown by electing him to the Council of Honor.

name: Fortner, James Dalton
description: "Fortner" hails from Dallas, wherever that place is; it can't be very far from here though, for he walked in. He is so fond of written lessons that he prefers that to any other kind of pastime. He can always be relied on to be with the class in everything, right or wrong. "Good for you, Fortner."
text: "Fortner" hails from Dallas, wherever that place is; it can't be very far from here though, for he walked in. He is so fond of written lessons that he prefers that to any other kind of pastime. He can always be relied on to be with the class in everything, right or wrong. "Good for you, Fortner."
